subject: [PATCH v4 0/2] mm/vmscan: reduce lru_lock contention via vmstat-derived scan-balance cost

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary:
The reviewer, Johannes Weiner, suggested simplifying the patch description by focusing on the key balance formula and explaining how it's derived from vmstat counters instead of directly recording costs.

Gave Acked-by
reviews.raw_body:
^^^

This is a quite a bit of explaining the code, which is kind of
drowning out what's really going on. How about something like:

---

The balance formula for anon and file, respectively, is this:

    cost = nr_io * SWAP_CLUSTER_MAX + nr_rotated

Instead of recording cost and running averaging logic directly when
these events occur, snapshot running vmstat counters once per reclaim
cycle and derive the balance from event deltas since the last run.

WORKINGSET_RESTORE_* and NR_VMSCAN_WRITE already exist and have been
accounted all along; PGROTATE_* are added for replacing the rotation
event callbacks.

This is overall cheaper and has fewer lock aquisition sites.

---

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton acknowledged the patch and mentioned that he will test it before providing a formal review, but did not raise any specific technical issues or concerns.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks.  We're still awaiting review of [2/2].  I'll get this under
test in order to parallelize things.

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt
reviews.summary: Reviewer Shakeel Butt noted that exposing pgrotate_[anon|file] metrics to userspace may cause confusion due to their different representations compared to older and newer metrics, and asked if it's necessary to expose them to userspace at this point. He suggested delaying userspace exposure until a more solid use case is established.
reviews.raw_body:
Oh man too long commit message, please follow Johannes's suggestion.

The patch looks good but I have one question. We already have concept of
pgrotated through /proc/vmstat which represents number of pages we have moved to
the tail of inactive LRU to make them next reclaim candidates. This patch is
exposing pgrotate_[anon|file] to userspace. Here these metrics represents either
the number of pages we scanned but didn't reclaim plus number of pages given one
more trip in the active LRU.

Older and newer pgrotate metrics kind of represent something different. It may
cause confusion. Now before suggesting to change the name, let me ask do we
really need to expose these to the userspace? How exactly users can use these
new metrics? I think you can use them here without exposing to userspace. So,
does it makes sense to delay userspace exposure later when we have a more solid
usecase for that unless you have it already.

BTW really awesome work and I really like how these patches have reduced the lru
lock contention drastically.

2026-07-23.patch_summary: This patch reduces contention on the lru_lock by moving the calculation of scan-balance costs from producers to consumers, using vmstat-derived counters instead of scalar values in struct lruvec. The patch introduces a dedicated cost_lock for per-lruvec accumulation and decay, improving the accuracy of the cost model across reclaim gaps.
